page: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g is a general-purpose server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration provides 32 vCPUs and 32 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, utilizing a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is limited to a 1 GB network-attached volume, and there are no onboard GPUs.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 3056.39, placing it in the top 25 percent of tested instances. Multi-core CPU performance is moderate, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 48789.2, positioning it in the middle 50 percent. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (specifically AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

Because this server uses a shared CPU allocation model, it offers a highly cost-effective option for workloads requiring a large memory footprint and high vCPU count without the premium cost of dedicated hardware. However, the shared CPU allocation represents a performance tradeoff, as CPU resources are shared with other tenants, which can introduce variability under sustained multi-core loads. This profile is suitable for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded applications with bursty CPU requirements, staging environments, and memory-intensive tasks that do not require continuous, dedicated CPU performance.
description: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g is a general-purpose server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 32 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz on an x86_64 architecture, this instance utilizes a shared CPU allocation model. It features a 1 GB network-attached local storage volume and lacks GPU acceleration. In benchmark evaluations, the server delivers strong single-core performance with a stress-ng score of 3056.39, alongside average multi-core performance scoring 48789.2. Supported instruction sets include AVX-512 and cryptographic extensions like AES and SHA_NI. This server offers a cost-effective option for memory-intensive applications, staging environments, and general-purpose workloads that can tolerate shared CPU resources.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g is a general-purpose server featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC-Turin processor. It offers strong single-core performance and shared CPU allocation for cost-effective scaling.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g is a general-purpose server with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC-Turin CPU, utilizing a shared allocation model.
tagline: A high-capacity shared-CPU instance balancing a large memory footprint with strong single-core performance for cost-effective general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC-Turin processor with 32 vCPUs and 128.0 GB RAM
Shared CPU allocation model for cost-efficient resource scaling
Strong single-core performance ranking in the top 25 percent of benchmarks
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and AES cryptography
1 GB network-attached local storage with no integrated GPU
Best suited for memory-intensive staging, testing, and bursty workloads
